top_categories.workout_context: 7,776ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic UCI Gran Fondo World Championships' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
At mile 60 of 87, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
89ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved mountain/hilly road course — profile varies. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
UCI Gran Fondo World Championships' paved mountain/hilly road course — profile varies forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 87 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of UCI Gran Fondo World Championships. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,776ft of climbing on paved mountain/hilly road course — profile varies does to your power file on race day.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 87 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
At UCI Gran Fondo World Championships, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At UCI Gran Fondo World Championships, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 87 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for UCI Gran Fondo World Championships’s 87 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at UCI Gran Fondo World Championships. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 87 miles of paved mountain/hilly road course — profile varies by venue in Venue rotates annually (2026: Niseko, Hokkaido, Japan).
